zh.lifeSummary: 當Dirk VI van Holland於1114年出生在荷蘭霍蘭丹 哈格時，他的父親Floris II van Holland29歲，母親Gertrude de Lorraine38歲。他於1135年與Sophie von Rheineck結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1157年8月5日在荷蘭武特雷特阿梅斯福爾特享年43歲，埋葬在荷蘭-拉藍德利斯包格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dirk VI van Holland于1114年出生在荷兰霍蘭丹 哈格时，他的父亲Floris II van Holland29岁，母亲Gertrude de Lorraine38岁。他于1135年与Sophie von Rheineck结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1157年8月5日在荷兰武特雷特阿梅斯福爾特享年43岁，埋葬在荷兰-拉藍德利斯包格。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Dirk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Flemish and Dutch form of Derek . Its use in the English-speaking world since the 1960s is largely due to the fame of the actor Dirk Bogarde ( 1921–99 , born Derek Niven van den Bogaerde ). He was of Dutch descent, although he was actually born in Scotland. The manly image of the name has been reinforced by its coincidence in form with Scottish dirk ‘dagger’ (from Gaelic durc).

nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Dirk VI van Holland was born in 1114, in The Hague, Holland, Netherlands, his father, Floris II van Holland, was 29 and his mother, Gertrude de Lorraine, was 38. He married Sophie von Rheineck in 1135. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 5 August 1157, in Amersfoort, Utrecht, Netherlands, at the age of 43, and was buried in Rijnsburg, South Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dirk VI van Holland est né en 1114, à La Haye, Holland, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris II van Holland, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Gertrude de Lorraine, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Sophie von Rheineck en 1135. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 5 août 1157, à Amersfoort, Utrecht, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Rijnsburg, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas.

de.Egmond aan den Hoef, Egmond-Binnen, Holl: Egmond aan den Hoef, Egmond-Binnen, Holland, Niederlande
de.lifeSummary: Als Dirk VI van Holland 1114 in Den Haag, Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Floris II van Holland 29 und seine Mutter Gertrude de Lorraine 38. Er hat 1135 Sophie von Rheineck geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 5. August 1157 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Amersfoort, Utrecht, Niederlande und wurde in Rijnsburg, Südholland, Niederlande begraben.

Lui sposato Sophie von Rheineck nel 1135. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 5 agosto 1157, nel Amersfoort, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Dirk VI van Hollandが1114年にオランダホーランドデン ハアグで生まれた時，父Floris II van Hollandは29歳，母Gertrude de Lorraineは38歳であった。1135年にSophie von Rheineckと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1157年8月5日にオランダウトレチトエイメルスフォオルトで43歳で死去し，オランダズイド-hオランドリジンスブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.Egmond aan den Hoef, Egmond-Binnen, Holl: 네덜란드 홀랜드 그몽-빈 그몽 아아 든 호프
ko.lifeSummary: Dirk VI van Holland(이)가 1114년 네덜란드 홀랜드 든 하아그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Floris II van Holland(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertrude de Lorraine(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1135년 Sophie von Rheineck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1157년 8월 5일 네덜란드 우트레ch트 어머스푸어트에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 리j스버그에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Sophie von Rheineck em 1135. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Egmond aan den Hoef, Egmond-Binnen, Holl: эгмонд аан ден хоеф, эгмонд-биннен, холланд, Нидерланды
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dirk VI van Holland родился в 1114 г. в местности ден хааг, холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Floris II van Holland было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gertrude de Lorraine, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Sophie von Rheineck в 1135 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 5 августа 1157 г. в местности амирсфурт, утречт, Нидерланды в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности риджнсбург, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды.

Se casó con Sophie von Rheineck en 1135. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 5 de agosto de 1157, en Amersfoort, Utrecht, Países Bajos, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Rijnsburg, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos.
